How to Prevent Common Household Pests

Roberto Cruz — March 10, 2026

Prevention is the best approach to pest control. By implementing simple strategies and maintaining good habits, you can significantly reduce the likelihood of pest infestations in your home.

Seal Entry Points

Inspect your home for cracks, gaps, and openings where pests can enter. Seal cracks in foundations, walls, and around windows and doors. Install door sweeps and repair damaged screens. Pay special attention to areas where utilities enter your home, as these are common entry points for rodents and insects.

Eliminate Food Sources

Store food in airtight containers and clean up spills immediately. Don't leave pet food out overnight, and keep garbage in sealed containers. Clean your kitchen regularly, including behind appliances and inside cabinets. Wipe down counters and sweep floors daily to remove crumbs and food particles.

Remove Standing Water

Mosquitoes and other pests breed in standing water. Empty birdbaths, flower pots, and other containers regularly. Fix leaky faucets and pipes, and ensure proper drainage around your home. Clean gutters to prevent water accumulation and reduce moisture in crawl spaces and basements.

Maintain Your Yard

Keep grass trimmed and bushes pruned away from your home's exterior. Remove leaf litter, fallen fruit, and yard debris where pests can hide. Store firewood at least 20 feet from your home and elevated off the ground. Trim tree branches that touch or overhang your roof.

Regular Cleaning and Decluttering

Clutter provides hiding places for pests. Regularly clean and organize storage areas, basements, and attics. Vacuum frequently, including under furniture and in corners. Keep storage items in sealed plastic containers rather than cardboard boxes, which pests can easily penetrate.

While these prevention strategies are highly effective, they work best when combined with professional pest control services. QUREIT Pest Control offers customized prevention programs that provide ongoing protection for your home. Contact us today to learn more about our preventive maintenance plans.
